breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Author Quietly Admits His Blockchain Proof Timestamps the Wrong File
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: On 2026-04-17 the dome author published methodology.json, which explicitly scopes OpenTimestamps to 'snapshot existence' and instructs AI auditors: 'Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective.' This is the exact distinction our Timestamp Error tab has drawn since it was written. The structural fix (predictions-only file + independent OTS anchor) is still not implemented.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Most direct rhetorical concession to date. The dome author is now telling his own AI auditors what our tab tells human readers — while the structural problem persists.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome's Own Taxonomy: Zero WINs Tagged 'Prospective Confirmed' — The Column Is Empty
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The new claim_index.json publishes partial WIN classifications using a five-class taxonomy the author defined himself. Every tagged WIN falls into non-prospective classes (retrospective_structural, pending_contested, supportive_nonunique). The only prospective_confirmed item is a pending eclipse prediction, not a confirmed WIN. The headline '69 confirmed' now sits above a taxonomy the author created that reserves 'genuine forecast' status for a column with zero entries.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: The dome author has created his own taxonomy that — applied consistently — destroys the '69 confirmed' headline. This is a self-contradiction using his own methodology, not ours.

exp_171_naming_collision_note: ALERT: EXP-171 ID collision. Expansion tracker has EXP-171 assigned to ISS-1073 (review-response.html analysis, status=pending). Analyst also wrote a file named EXP-171-WIN-015-nearfield-calc.json with id=EXP-171 covering WIN-015 near-field calculation. Both claim the same ID. The WIN-015 file is a no_op (EXP-168 already integrated the calculation). The tracker's EXP-171 (ISS-1073 review-response analysis) remains pending. No collision in the actual data since the WIN-015 work is a no_op and not being integrated. Tinker should audit this for process improvement.
